title: Crunchy Fermented Syrian Rice Paper Sheets Spring-Rolls
description: A midnight-craving lacto-vegetarian-friendly dish featuring crispy rice paper sheets and a tangy fermented flavor. Serve immediately for the best experience.

ingredients.name: Rice paper sheets
ingredients.notes: Look for sheets that are fermented for a few days to get the right flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a salt block to make the salt more flavorful.

ingredients.name: Vinegar
ingredients.notes: Apple cider vinegar or white wine vinegar work well.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Use a bit of ginger for extra flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds a nice aroma.

ingredients.name: Scallions
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped scallions add a nice crunch.

ingredients.name: Mung bean paste
ingredients.notes: This adds a nice fermented flavor to the spring rolls.

ingredients.name: Pumpkin seeds
ingredients.notes: Toasted pumpkin seeds add a nice crunch.

instructions: Soak the rice paper sheets in water for a few seconds to make them pliable.
Cut the scallions and ginger into fine pieces.
In a pan, heat the mung bean paste over medium heat until it's fragrant.
Add the vinegar and stir well.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir for a few seconds.
Add the pumpkin seeds and stir well.
Place a rice paper sheet on a flat surface and put a few tablespoons of the mung bean paste mixture in the center of the sheet.
Fold the bottom half of the sheet over the filling, and then fold in the sides and roll up the sheet to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
